title: fd-based FUSE passthrough with BackingFs trait abstraction
description:
Refactor the FUSE passthrough layer to use a pre-opened directory fd and *at() syscalls, abstracted behind a BackingFs trait. This enables mounting FUSE directly over a versioned repository (git/pijul/jj) without the recursive re-entry deadlock, and without requiring a separate working/ directory copy.

## Architecture

```
User I/O → FUSE kernel → ObservingFS<FdPassthroughFS>
                              │
                              ├─ VCS dirs (.git/.pijul/.jj): passthrough only, no recording
                              └─ Everything else: passthrough + observe/record
                                    │
                                    ▼
                              FdPassthroughFS
                                    │ (delegates all I/O)
                                    ▼
                              BackingFs trait
                                    │
                                    ▼
                              LibcBackingFs (raw libc *at() on pre-opened base_fd)
```

## Key design decisions

1. **BackingFs trait** in `backing_fs/mod.rs` — abstracts directory-relative FS ops (stat, open, read, write, mkdir, unlink, rename, etc.)
2. **LibcBackingFs** in `backing_fs/libc.rs` — concrete impl using raw libc `openat/fstatat/mkdirat/unlinkat/renameat` against a pre-opened base_fd
3. **FdPassthroughFS** in `filesystems/passthrough_fd.rs` — FUSE Filesystem impl that uses BackingFs, manages inode→path mapping and file handle table
4. **VCS detection** — detect .git/.pijul/.jj at mount time, filter observer notifications so VCS dirs are passthrough-only
5. **fuse-fd-poc rewrite** — update PoC binary to use library types for validation

## Validated by

The fuse-fd-poc binary (already proven working) demonstrates the base_fd approach works. This epic extracts that into proper library architecture.

title: Create BackingFs trait (backing_fs/mod.rs)
Implement LibcBackingFs (backing_fs/libc.rs)
description:
Implement the `BackingFs` trait using raw libc `*at()` syscalls against a pre-opened directory fd.

## File: `ize-lib/src/backing_fs/libc_impl.rs`

## Struct

```rust
pub struct LibcBackingFs {
    base_fd: RawFd,  // pre-opened with O_RDONLY | O_DIRECTORY before FUSE mount
}
```

## Syscall mapping

| Trait method  | libc call                                  |
|---------------|--------------------------------------------|
| stat          | fstatat(base_fd, rel, AT_SYMLINK_NOFOLLOW) |
| fstat         | fstat(fd)                                  |
| open_file     | openat(base_fd, rel, flags, mode)          |
| pread         | pread(fd, buf, len, offset)                |
| pwrite        | pwrite(fd, data, len, offset)              |
| fsync         | fsync(fd)                                  |
| close_fd      | close(fd)                                  |
| ftruncate     | ftruncate(fd, size)                        |
| mkdir         | mkdirat(base_fd, rel, mode)                |
| rmdir         | unlinkat(base_fd, rel, AT_REMOVEDIR)       |
| readdir       | openat+dup+fdopendir+readdir+closedir      |
| unlink        | unlinkat(base_fd, rel, 0)                  |
| rename        | renameat(base_fd, old, base_fd, new)       |
| chmod         | fchmodat(base_fd, rel, mode, 0)            |
| chown         | fchownat(base_fd, rel, uid, gid, AT_SYMLINK_NOFOLLOW) |
| utimens       | utimensat(base_fd, rel, times, AT_SYMLINK_NOFOLLOW)    |
| access        | faccessat(base_fd, rel, mask, AT_SYMLINK_NOFOLLOW)     |
| statvfs       | fstatvfs(base_fd)                          |

## Critical property

The base_fd is opened BEFORE the FUSE mount is established. The kernel resolves it to the underlying filesystem's inode. All `*at()` calls through it bypass the FUSE layer entirely — no recursive re-entry.

## Helper functions

- `rel_cpath(rel: &Path) -> io::Result<CString>` — convert Path to C string, empty path becomes "."
- `LibcBackingFs::new(base_fd: RawFd) -> Self`
- Consider: `open_dir_for(base_fd, rel) -> io::Result<RawFd>` for readdir

## Lifecycle

- Caller owns the base_fd lifetime
- `LibcBackingFs` does NOT close base_fd on drop (caller responsibility)
- Individual file fds from open_file ARE caller's responsibility via close_fd

## Source

Extract from the validated fuse-fd-poc PoC (sys_openat, sys_fstatat, sys_fstat, sys_mkdirat, sys_unlinkat, sys_renameat, sys_readdir functions).

title: Create FdPassthroughFS (filesystems/passthrough_fd.rs)
description:
Create `FdPassthroughFS` — a FUSE `Filesystem` implementation that delegates all underlying I/O through the `BackingFs` trait.

## File: `ize-lib/src/filesystems/passthrough_fd.rs`

## Struct

```rust
pub struct FdPassthroughFS {
    backing: Box<dyn BackingFs>,
    inode_to_path: InodeMap,           // Arc<RwLock<HashMap<u64, PathBuf>>>
    next_fh: AtomicU64,
    open_files: RwLock<HashMap<u64, OpenFile>>,
    read_only: bool,
    vcs_dirs: Vec<OsString>,           // detected VCS dirs (.git, .pijul, .jj)
    mount_point: PathBuf,              // informational only
}
```

## FUSE methods to implement

All methods from the existing PassthroughFS, but using `self.backing` instead of path-based std::fs calls:

| FUSE method | BackingFs call(s)                              |
|-------------|------------------------------------------------|
| lookup      | backing.stat(child_rel)                        |
| getattr     | backing.stat(rel) or backing.fstat(fd)         |
| setattr     | backing.ftruncate / backing.chmod / backing.chown / backing.utimens + backing.stat |
| readdir     | backing.readdir(rel)                           |
| open        | backing.open_file(rel, flags, 0)               |
| read        | backing.pread(fd, buf, offset)                 |
| write       | backing.pwrite(fd, data, offset)               |
| create      | backing.open_file(rel, flags|O_CREAT, mode) + backing.fstat |
| mkdir       | backing.mkdir(rel, mode) + backing.stat        |
| unlink      | backing.unlink(rel)                            |
| rmdir       | backing.rmdir(rel)                             |
| rename      | backing.rename(old, new)                       |
| flush       | backing.fsync(fd)                              |
| release     | backing.close_fd(fd) + remove from handle table |
| fsync       | backing.fsync(fd)                              |
| access      | backing.access(rel, mask)                      |
| statfs      | backing.statvfs()                              |
| opendir     | just verify inode exists                       |
| releasedir  | noop                                           |

## VCS directory detection

On construction, scan the root directory for .git, .pijul, .jj directories and store in `vcs_dirs`. Expose:

```rust
pub fn is_vcs_path(&self, rel_path: &Path) -> bool
pub fn detected_vcs(&self) -> &[OsString]
pub fn inode_map(&self) -> InodeMap  // for sharing with observers
```

## Inode mapping

Same approach as existing PassthroughFS — use real inodes from underlying FS, lazily populate during lookup()/readdir(). FUSE root inode 1 maps to empty relative path.

## File handle table

Same RAII approach as existing PassthroughFS — monotonic fh counter, HashMap<u64, OpenFile> where OpenFile holds the raw fd + rel_path + flags. On release(), remove from map and close_fd().

## Compatibility

Should work as a drop-in replacement for PassthroughFS inside ObservingFS<F>. The ObservingFS wrapper can check is_vcs_path() to skip notifications for VCS directories.

title: VCS directory detection and observer filtering
description:
Implement the logic that detects which VCS backend is in use and filters observer notifications so that operations on VCS directories (.git, .pijul, .jj) are passed through but NOT recorded.

## Detection

On FdPassthroughFS construction, scan the root via `backing.readdir("")` for known VCS directories:

| Directory | VCS backend |
|-----------|-------------|
| .git      | Git         |
| .pijul    | Pijul       |
| .jj       | Jujutsu     |

Store detected names in `vcs_dirs: Vec<OsString>`.

## Filtering

`FdPassthroughFS::is_vcs_path(rel: &Path) -> bool` checks if the first component of `rel` is in `vcs_dirs`.

Two options for where filtering happens:

### Option A: In ObservingFS (recommended)
ObservingFS gains access to the inner FdPassthroughFS's `is_vcs_path()` and skips observer notification for VCS paths. Clean separation — passthrough layer doesn't know about observers.

### Option B: In the observer (OpcodeRecorder)
The observer checks paths before recording. Simpler to implement but pushes VCS awareness into the wrong layer.

### Option C: In FdPassthroughFS itself
FdPassthroughFS could hold observers directly (bypassing ObservingFS). Less modular.

## Recommendation

Go with Option A. Add a method to ObservingFS:
```rust
impl<F: Filesystem> ObservingFS<F> {
    pub fn set_path_filter(&mut self, filter: Box<dyn Fn(&Path) -> bool + Send + Sync>);
}
```
Or more specifically for the VCS case, accept a reference to the vcs_dirs list.

## Edge cases

- Nested VCS dirs (e.g. submodules with their own .git) — first component check handles this naturally
- VCS dirs created/deleted after mount — should we rescan? For MVP, detect at mount time only
- Operations ON the VCS dir itself (e.g. mkdir .git) — should be passthrough-only

title: Wire up backing_fs and passthrough_fd modules
Rewrite fuse-fd-poc to use library BackingFs + FdPassthroughFS
